title: how to use `t_end_flag` in `TRANSITIVE` clause?
description:
Virtuoso SPARQL allows access to Virtuoso's SQL transitivity extension.
option (transitive transitivity_option[,...])

  transitivity_option ::=  t_in (<variable_list>)
  | t_out (<variable_list>)
  | t_distinct
  | t_shortest_only
  | t_no_cycles
  | t_cycles_only
  | t_min (INTNUM)
  | t_max (INTNUM)
  | t_end_flag (<variable>)
  | t_step (<variiable_or_step>)
  | t_direction INTNUM

  variable_list ::= <variable> [,...]

  variable_or_step ::= <variable> | path_id' | 'step_no'

But don't give any description or sample about t_end_flag
please tell me something about this
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_transitive (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT, value REAL, created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_transitive (id, name, value) VALUES (1, 'Node A', 10.5), (2, 'Node B', 20.0), (3, 'Node C', NULL);
UPDATE test_transitive SET value = value + 5 WHERE id = 1;
SELECT name FROM test_transitive WHERE value IS NOT NULL AND created_at > DATETIME('now', '-1 day');

SELECT x, syn FROM (SELECT x, syn FROM (SELECT id AS x, name AS syn FROM test_transitive WHERE id IN (1, 2, 3)) UNION SELECT syn, x FROM (SELECT id AS syn, name AS x FROM test_transitive WHERE id IN (1, 2, 3))) WHERE x = 'Node A';
key_bug_features: Using t_end_flag in TRANSITIVE clause causes issues
